message:
fix(security): tighten Netlify Function CORS origins (#9879) (#9910)

OWASP ZAP baseline scan on console.kubestellar.io flagged a Medium-severity
"Cross-Domain Misconfiguration" finding. The root cause: nine Netlify
Functions served with a blanket `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`. Several
console hooks (auth, rewards, persistence, version-check) send requests
with `credentials: 'include'`, and `*` combined with credentialed
cross-origin fetches is a known CORS-leakage pattern.

Changes
-------

- Add `web/netlify/functions/_shared/cors.ts` — a single place defining
  the allowlist (console.kubestellar.io, Netlify previews,
  localhost:5173/5174/8080/8888) and helpers (`buildCorsHeaders`,
  `handlePreflight`, `isAllowedOrigin`). The helper also emits
  `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` on every response and `Vary: Origin`
  so shared caches key by origin — both address related Low-severity ZAP
  findings on the same endpoints.

- Replace `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` with allowlisted, per-request
  origin echoing in nine console-internal Netlify Functions:
  nightly-e2e, github-rewards, analytics-dashboard, issue-stats,
  missions-browse, missions-file, missions-scores, feedback-app,
  umami-collect.

- Leave `acmm-badge.mts` as `*` on purpose — it is a public,
  unauthenticated, shields.io-embeddable badge endpoint that must be
  fetchable from arbitrary README hosts. Added a comment explaining
  why this one should NOT be tightened.

Addresses OWASP ZAP CWE-264 (Cross-Domain Misconfiguration, rule 10098)
and partially addresses the X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing
(rule 10021) Low finding on the affected endpoints.

Fixes #9879

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -85,6 +85,11 @@ function corsHeaders(
   const cacheControl = withSWR
     ? `public, max-age=${cacheSeconds}, stale-while-revalidate=86400`
     : `public, max-age=${cacheSeconds}`;
+  // This is a public, unauthenticated, embeddable badge endpoint — the `*`
+  // CORS is intentional so shields.io and any README host (github.com,
+  // raw.githubusercontent.com, pkg.go.dev, crates.io, etc.) can fetch it.
+  // Do NOT tighten this origin (see web/netlify/functions/_shared/cors.ts
+  // for the tightened console-internal endpoints per #9879).
   const headers: Record<string, string> = {
     "Cache-Control": cacheControl,
     "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
